text: Trump tells the UK and other countries 'go get your own oil' from Strait of Hormuz and 'just take it' - BBC
fullText: The US president tells nations who are now unable to get jet fuel "the USA won't be there to help you anymore, just like you weren't there for us".

text: Stock Market Today: Oil Pauses, Dow Futures Rise — Live Updates - WSJ
fullText: Live updates on markets and the top finance, economics and business stories. Plus the latest on oil prices, the Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq.

text: Iran Attacks Oil Tanker After Trump Wavers on War Escalation - Bloomberg.com
fullText: Iran hit a fully laden Kuwaiti oil tanker off the coast of Dubai during a fresh wave of attacks around the Persian Gulf, a sign it’s willing to escalate strikes on energy assets as the war drags on.

text: Trump floats siege on Iran stronghold as Tehran loosens grip on Strait of Hormuz - Fox News
fullText: President Donald Trump suggested the U.S. may seize Iran's Kharg Island on Monday as Tehran allowed 20 cargo ships to travel through the Strait of Hormuz.

text: Euro zone inflation smashes through ECB target to 2.5% in March as energy costs soar - CNBC
fullText: The increase largely reflects a sharp jump in energy prices since the U.S. and Israel launched their military operation against Iran.

text: Babies are an afterthought in the birthright citizenship case, advocates say - NPR
fullText: An end to birthright citizenship would mean a new layer of bureaucracy for all babies born in the U.S. and could cause delays for health insurance and other benefits.

text: Do you have the new Covid variant, flu or other virus? Look out for these symptoms - NBC News
fullText: Flu and Covid, including a new variant called BA.3.2, nicknamed “cicada,” are still circulating across the U.S. in 2026. How do you know what's making you sick?

text: Haiti: At least 70 killed in 'massacre,' says rights group - DW.com
fullText: The death toll from a brutal gang attack in Haiti is much higher than initially thought, according to a human rights group.

text: The DHS shutdown might never end - Politico
fullText: The strongest impetus for a deal — the hourslong security lines at some U.S. airports — is already dissipating.

text: Another Starlink satellite has inexplicably exploded - The Verge
fullText: SpaceX says it lost contact with a Starlink satellite after suffering an “anomaly.” Leo Labs says it “immediately detected tens of objects in the vicinity.”

text: Get ready for kitsch and drama, Asia. Pop extravaganza Eurovision is coming your way - CNN
fullText: The world-famous TV pop spectacle known as much for its elaborate costumes and theatrical performances as it is for its political controversies is setting up stage on a new continent.

text: Rapper Future’s Ex Brittni Mealy Demands He Be Thrown in Jail - TMZ
fullText: Rapper Future’s ex-girlfriend, Brittni Mealy, demanded he be thrown behind bars for failing to comply with a court order over their 12-year-old son, Prince, TMZ has learned.

text: Yankees execute perfect and aggressive ABS challenge night —and let the ump hear about it - New York Post
fullText: That happened in the fourth inning, when Ben Rice, Giancarlo Stanton and Jazz Chisholm Jr. each successfully challenged pitches below the zone in consecutive at-bats.

text: Trump unveils renderings of planned presidential library tower - The Washington Post
fullText: The president and his son Eric shared images of a towering skyscraper that would be located in downtown Miami.

text: Seizing Kharg Island would risk US troops’ lives and may not end Iran war, experts say - AP News

text: Apple introduces privacy rules for third-party access to notifications and Live Activities - 9to5Mac
fullText: Apple has updated its Developer Program License Agreement with new rules on how third-party accessories must handle forwarded notifications.

text: Live Updates: Trump renews threat to Iran's power plants as war sends oil prices soaring again - CBS News
fullText: Energy markets remain volatile as President Trump threatens Iran with an invasion to seize its oil while also suggesting a deal could soon end the war.

text: TSA agents see partial paychecks - Federal News Network
fullText: While most TSA employees should have received at least partial backpay on Monday, thousands more DHS employees continue to work without pay.
